description: The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

description: User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Client IP Tracker?

Feature Caveat: Client IP Tracker - User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel in AOS-CX 10.13.1150?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. To prevent this cycle, disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is a chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique ports for each service across the system.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what RADIUS caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for REST?
REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: UBT VLAN local mode does not support multiple MAC addresses or clients in same VLAN on the same switch port, as this configuration can cause mac-move issues on an L2 switch connected to a UBT Switch. Use UBT vlan-extend mode to support multiple MAC address or clients in a differently tagged VLAN on a single switchport.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for UBT Design?

Feature Caveat: UBT Design - UBT VLAN local mode does not support multiple MAC addresses or clients in the same VLAN on the same switch port, as this configuration can cause mac-move issues on an L2 switch connected to a UBT Switch. Use UBT vlan-extend mode to support multiple MAC addresses or clients in differently tagged VLANs on a single switchport.

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

Certification: Refer to the Approved Product Lists sites for the Common Criteria, FIPS 140-2 and DoDIN APL to obtain the product certification details. Products should be used as evaluated and defined in the respective configuration guides. section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, RL.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, RL.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, for 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, RL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, for 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what are the steps to perform a software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

description: Symptom: STP ports are entering into Disabled/Blocking state. Scenario: This issue occurs after an edge device log-off and log-in back to a secured port or when a server connected to VSX pair is rebooted. Workaround: Reboot the PVST process.
